agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a standard e-commerce/logistics capability allowing customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment completes. The PM's requirement is stated without specifics on timing windows, refund policies, partial cancellation, or order lifecycle constraints — all of which are critical to a correct implementation.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed request by a customer to purchase one or more items, progressing through a defined lifecycle of states from placement to completion."},{"term":"Order Cancellation","definition":"A customer- or system-initiated action that terminates an order, halting further fulfillment and triggering any applicable refund or reversal processes."},{"term":"Order State","definition":"The current phase of an order's lifecycle (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period during which an order is eligible for cancellation — typically constrained by how far fulfillment has progressed."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer following a cancellation, subject to refund policy rules."},{"term":"Partial Cancellation","definition":"Cancelling specific line items from a multi-item order while the remaining items continue through fulfillment."},{"term":"Fulfillment","definition":"The warehouse/logistics process of picking, packing, and shipping an order to the customer."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled while it is in a cancellable state (before a fulfillment-irreversibility threshold is reached).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancelling an order that has already been physically shipped or delivered would corrupt the fulfillment pipeline — you cannot un-ship a package. State integrity requires this gate.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request for an order in a non-cancellable state (e.g., Shipped, Delivered) MUST be rejected with an explanatory error."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Only the customer who placed the order (or an authorized agent) may cancel it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unauthorized cancellation violates data ownership and could be exploited for fraud or denial-of-service against other customers.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request where the requesting user ID does not match the order's owner (and the user has no admin/agent role) MUST be rejected with a 403/unauthorized response."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A cancelled order MUST NOT proceed further through the fulfillment pipeline.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If a cancelled order continues to be picked, packed, or shipped, the system state is corrupt — resources are wasted and the customer receives unwanted goods.","testable_assertion":"After successful cancellation, no downstream fulfillment events (pick, pack, ship) may be recorded against that order."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Cancellation MUST trigger the applicable refund process according to refund policy.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The refund mechanism and timing are policy decisions (immediate vs. delayed, full vs. partial) that business leadership can change. But the rule that some refund process is initiated is a hard requirement.","testable_assertion":"When an order is successfully cancelled, a refund event MUST be emitted or a refund record created within the system."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The order's state MUST transition to Cancelled and this transition MUST be persisted atomically with any side effects (inventory restoration, refund initiation).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A non-atomic transition could leave the system in a half-cancelled state — e.g., inventory restored but order still marked Processing, or order marked Cancelled but payment not refunded.","testable_assertion":"The order state change to Cancelled, inventory adjustment, and refund initiation MUST succeed or fail as a unit — no partial application."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Cancelled inventory MUST be restored to available stock.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If reserved inventory is not released on cancellation, the system shows phantom stock depletion, leading to false out-of-stock conditions.","testable_assertion":"After cancellation, the quantity of each cancelled line item MUST be added back to available inventory."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has an order in 'Placed' or 'Confirmed' state","when":"The customer requests cancellation of that order","then":"The order transitions to 'Cancelled', fulfillment is halted, inventory is restored, a refund is initiated, and the customer receives a cancellation confirmation."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer has an order in 'Shipped' or 'Delivered' state","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the request with a clear message explaining the order is no longer cancellable and suggests the returns process instead."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an order in 'Processing' state (being picked/packed)","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The system attempts cancellation; if fulfillment has not reached an irreversible step, the order is cancelled. If it has, the request is rejected with an explanation."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A user who did NOT place the order and has no admin/agent role","when":"They attempt to cancel the order","then":"The system rejects the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order has already been cancelled","when":"A cancellation request is submitted again for the same order","then":"The system returns an idempotent success or a clear message that the order is already cancelled — no duplicate refunds or inventory adjustments occur."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A multi-item order in a cancellable state","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"All line items are cancelled, inventory is restored for each item, and the full refund is initiated (partial cancellation behavior is undefined in current spec — see escalations)."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A cancellation is successfully processed","when":"The system completes the cancellation","then":"An audit trail entry is created recording who cancelled, when, and the order's previous state."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A cancellation request is submitted but a transient failure occurs (e.g., payment gateway timeout)","when":"The refund step fails","then":"The order MUST NOT be marked Cancelled; the system retries or flags for manual intervention, preserving atomicity."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: order transitions from Confirmed to Shipped while cancellation is in flight","expected_behavior":"The cancellation must re-check order state at commit time. If the state has changed to a non-cancellable state, the cancellation fails and the customer is informed. Optimistic locking or state version checks prevent a stale cancellation from succeeding."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels during a payment authorization hold (not yet captured)","expected_behavior":"The authorization hold must be voided rather than refunded, as no capture has occurred. The system must distinguish between void and refund paths."},{"scenario":"Promotional or discounted order is cancelled","expected_behavior":"Refund amount must reflect what the customer actually paid (after discounts/coupons), not the list price. Coupon/promo code reuse policy must be applied (e.g., is the coupon restored for future use?)."},{"scenario":"Order paid with multiple payment methods (split tender)","expected_behavior":"Refund must be apportioned correctly across each payment method according to what was charged to each."},{"scenario":"Order includes a non-refundable item or service fee","expected_behavior":"The system must apply the refund policy per line item; non-refundable components are excluded from the refund and the customer is informed."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested during a flash sale with limited inventory","expected_behavior":"Restored inventory becomes immediately available, potentially allowing other customers to purchase. No artificial hold period on restored stock."},{"scenario":"Subscription or recurring order cancellation","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether 'cancel order' applies to a single occurrence or the entire subscription. Must be clarified."},{"scenario":"Third-party marketplace item within the order","expected_behavior":"Cancellation may require coordination with the third-party seller's cancellation policy, which may differ from the platform's policy."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Order cancellation directly affects revenue recognition, refund processing costs, and payment gateway fees (which may not be recoverable). A poorly implemented cancellation flow could lead to double refunds, lost inventory, or unrefunded cancellations — all direct financial losses.","operational":"Cancellation must integrate with warehouse management (halt picking), inventory systems (restock), payment systems (refund), and notification systems (customer comms). A failure in any integration creates manual intervention workload.","user":"Cancellation is a trust-building feature. Customers who can easily cancel feel safer placing orders. However, unclear cancellation windows or rejected cancellations with poor messaging erode trust and increase support volume.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) double-refund exploits if idempotency is not enforced, (2) inventory corruption if restock fails silently, (3) regulatory exposure if refunds are not processed within legally mandated timeframes (varies by jurisdiction), (4) race conditions leading to shipped-but-cancelled orders.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain self-service cancellation capability, reducing dependency on support channels. Experience depends on clarity of cancellation windows and refund timelines."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced inbound volume for cancellation requests if self-service works well. Need visibility into cancellation status and override capabilities for edge cases."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Must receive and act on cancellation signals in real-time to avoid picking/packing orders that are already cancelled. Requires system integration."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile refunds against revenue. Need audit trail for all cancellations. Refund timing affects cash flow reporting."},{"group":"Product/Business","impact":"Cancellation rate becomes a key metric. High cancellation rates may indicate UX or product-market fit issues."}]},"confidence":{"level":42,"reasoning":"The PM's requirement is a single sentence with no specifics on cancellation windows, refund policy, partial cancellation support, order lifecycle states, or authorization rules. Every critical business decision must be assumed. The spec quality is very low — nearly every acceptance criterion required domain inference rather than explicit requirements.","high_confidence_areas":["An order that has been physically shipped cannot be cancelled — this is a physical constraint","Cancellation must be authorized to the order owner","Cancelled orders must not continue through fulfillment","Inventory must be restored on cancellation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact set of cancellable order states — is 'Processing' cancellable?","Whether partial cancellation of line items is supported","Refund policy details: immediate vs delayed, full vs partial, non-refundable items","Coupon/promo restoration policy on cancellation","Whether this applies to subscription/recurring orders","Legally mandated refund timeframes by jurisdiction"],"assumptions":["Order has a defined state machine with at least: Placed, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled","Cancellation is full-order only (no partial line-item cancellation) since the spec does not mention it","Refund is initiated immediately on cancellation (policy details TBD)","Only the order owner or authorized agents can cancel"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order states are cancellable. Is an order in 'Processing' (being picked in the warehouse) still cancellable? The answer depends on warehouse integration capabilities and business policy.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Customer Support"],"options":["Cancellable only before Processing begins","Cancellable up until a shipping label is generated","Cancellable at any pre-ship state with best-effort warehouse halt"],"recommendation":"Define cancellable states explicitly with the fulfillment team. Recommend: cancellable up until shipping label generation, with best-effort halt during Processing."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No refund policy is specified. When is the refund issued? Is it always a full refund? Are there restocking fees? What about non-refundable items or services?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Full refund always, immediate","Full refund with processing delay","Partial refund with restocking fee for certain states","Per-item refund policy (some items non-refundable)"],"recommendation":"Clarify with Finance. Recommend: full refund for cancellations before Processing; refund minus restocking fee if cancellation occurs during Processing."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial cancellation (cancelling individual line items from a multi-item order) is not addressed. This is a fundamentally different feature with significant complexity.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Full order cancellation only in v1","Support partial cancellation from day one"],"recommendation":"Ship full-order cancellation only in v1. Partial cancellation is a separate feature requiring its own spec for refund apportionment, shipping cost recalculation, and inventory handling."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No mention of cancellation time window or cutoff. Some businesses allow cancellation only within N minutes of placement; others allow it until shipment.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Support","Product/Business"],"options":["Time-based window (e.g., 30 minutes after placement)","State-based window (until shipment)","Hybrid: time-based for guaranteed cancellation, state-based for best-effort"],"recommendation":"State-based cutoff is more intuitive for customers and simpler to implement. Recommend: cancellable until shipping label is generated."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The PM's requirement is stated without specifics on timing windows, refund policies, partial cancellation, or order lifecycle constraints — all of which are critical to a correct implementation.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed request by a customer to p

content.thinking: The PM says "Users should be able to cancel their order." This is vague — no specifics about timing, refund policy, order states, etc. This is ambiguity.

The PM's requirement is stated without specifics on timing windows, refund policies, partial cancellation, or order lifecycle constraints — all of which are critical to a correct implementation.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed request by a customer to purchase one or more items, progressing through a defined lifecycle of states from placement to completion."},{"term":"Order Cancellation","definition":"A customer- or system-initiated action that terminates an order, halting further fulfillment and triggering any applicable refund or reversal processes."},{"term":"Order State","definition":"The current phase of an order's lifecycle (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period during which an order is eligible for cancellation — typically constrained by how far fulfillment has progressed."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer following a cancellation, subject to refund policy rules."},{"term":"Partial Cancellation","definition":"Cancelling specific line items from a multi-item order while the remaining items continue through fulfillment."},{"term":"Fulfillment","definition":"The warehouse/logistics process of picking, packing, and shipping an order to the customer."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled while it is in a cancellable state (before a fulfillment-irreversibility threshold is reached).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancelling an order that has already been physically shipped or delivered would corrupt the fulfillment pipeline — you cannot un-ship a package. But the rule that some refund process is initiated is a hard requirement.","testable_assertion":"When an order is successfully cancelled, a refund event MUST be emitted or a refund record created within the system."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The order's state MUST transition to Cancelled and this transition MUST be persisted atomically with any side effects (inventory restoration, refund initiation).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A non-atomic transition could leave the system in a half-cancelled state — e.g., inventory restored but order still marked Processing, or order marked Cancelled but payment not refunded.","testable_assertion":"The order state change to Cancelled, inventory adjustment, and refund initiation MUST succeed or fail as a unit — no partial application."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Cancelled inventory MUST be restored to available stock.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If reserved inventory is not released on cancellation, the system shows phantom stock depletion, leading to false out-of-stock conditions.","testable_assertion":"After cancellation, the quantity of each cancelled line item MUST be added back to available inventory."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has an order in 'Placed' or 'Confirmed' state","when":"The customer requests cancellation of that order","then":"The order transitions to 'Cancelled', fulfillment is halted, inventory is restored, a refund is initiated, and the customer receives a cancellation confirmation."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer has an order in 'Shipped' or 'Delivered' state","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the request with a clear message explaining the order is no longer cancellable and suggests the returns process instead."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an order in 'Processing' state (being picked/packed)","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"The system attempts cancellation; if fulfillment has not reached an irreversible step, the order is cancelled. If it has, the request is rejected with an explanation."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A user who did NOT place the order and has no admin/agent role","when":"They attempt to cancel the order","then":"The system rejects the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order has already been cancelled","when":"A cancellation request is submitted again for the same order","then":"The system returns an idempotent success or a clear message that the order is already cancelled — no duplicate refunds or inventory adjustments occur."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A multi-item order in a cancellable state","when":"The customer requests cancellation","then":"All line items are cancelled, inventory is restored for each item, and the full refund is initiated (partial cancellation behavior is undefined in current spec — see escalations)."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A cancellation is successfully processed","when":"The system completes the cancellation","then":"An audit trail entry is created recording who cancelled, when, and the order's previous state."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A cancellation request is submitted but a transient failure occurs (e.g., payment gateway timeout)","when":"The refund step fails","then":"The order MUST NOT be marked Cancelled; the system retries or flags for manual intervention, preserving atomicity."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: order transitions from Confirmed to Shipped while cancellation is in flight","expected_behavior":"The cancellation must re-check order state at commit time. If the state has changed to a non-cancellable state, the cancellation fails and the customer is informed. Optimistic locking or state version checks prevent a stale cancellation from succeeding."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels during a payment authorization hold (not yet captured)","expected_behavior":"The authorization hold must be voided rather than refunded, as no capture has occurred. The system must distinguish between void and refund paths."},{"scenario":"Promotional or discounted order is cancelled","expected_behavior":"Refund amount must reflect what the customer actually paid (after discounts/coupons), not the list price. Must be clarified."},{"scenario":"Third-party marketplace item within the order","expected_behavior":"Cancellation may require coordination with the third-party seller's cancellation policy, which may differ from the platform's policy."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Order cancellation directly affects revenue recognition, refund processing costs, and payment gateway fees (which may not be recoverable). A poorly implemented cancellation flow could lead to double refunds, lost inventory, or unrefunded cancellations — all direct financial losses.","operational":"Cancellation must integrate with warehouse management (halt picking), inventory systems (restock), payment systems (refund), and notification systems (customer comms). Need visibility into cancellation status and override capabilities for edge cases."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Must receive and act on cancellation signals in real-time to avoid picking/packing orders that are already cancelled. Requires system integration."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile refunds against revenue. Need audit trail for all cancellations. Refund timing affects cash flow reporting."},{"group":"Product/Business","impact":"Cancellation rate becomes a key metric. High cancellation rates may indicate UX or product-market fit issues."}]},"confidence":{"level":42,"reasoning":"The PM's requirement is a single sentence with no specifics on cancellation windows, refund policy, partial cancellation support, order lifecycle states, or authorization rules. Every critical business decision must be assumed. The spec quality is very low — nearly every acceptance criterion required domain inference rather than explicit requirements.","high_confidence_areas":["An order that has been physically shipped cannot be cancelled — this is a physical constraint","Cancellation must be authorized to the order owner","Cancelled orders must not continue through fulfillment","Inventory must be restored on cancellation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact set of cancellable order states — is 'Processing' cancellable?","Whether partial cancellation of line items is supported","Refund policy details: immediate vs delayed, full vs partial, non-refundable items","Coupon/promo restoration policy on cancellation","Whether this applies to subscription/recurring orders","Legally mandated refund timeframes by jurisdiction"],"assumptions":["Order has a defined state machine with at least: Placed, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled","Cancellation is full-order only (no partial line-item cancellation) since the spec does not mention it","Refund is initiated immediately on cancellation (policy details TBD)","Only the order owner or authorized agents can cancel"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order states are cancellable. Is an order in 'Processing' (being picked in the warehouse) still cancellable? The answer depends on warehouse integration capabilities and business policy.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Customer Support"],"options":["Cancellable only before Processing begins","Cancellable up until a shipping label is generated","Cancellable at any pre-ship state with best-effort warehouse halt"],"recommendation":"Define cancellable states explicitly with the fulfillment team. Recommend: cancellable up until shipping label generation, with best-effort halt during Processing."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No refund policy is specified. When is the refund issued? Is it always a full refund? Are there restocking fees? What about non-refundable items or services?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Full refund always, immediate","Full refund with processing delay","Partial refund with restocking fee for certain states","Per-item refund policy (some items non-refundable)"],"recommendation":"Clarify with Finance. Recommend: full refund for cancellations before Processing; refund minus restocking fee if cancellation occurs during Processing."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial cancellation (cancelling individual line items from a multi-item order) is not addressed. This is a fundamentally different feature with significant complexity.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Full order cancellation only in v1","Support partial cancellation from day one"],"recommendation":"Ship full-order cancellation only in v1. Partial cancellation is a separate feature requiring its own spec for refund apportionment, shipping cost recalculation, and inventory handling."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No mention of cancellation time window or cutoff. Some businesses allow cancellation only within N minutes of placement; others allow it until shipment.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Support","Product/Business"],"options":["Time-based window (e.g., 30 minutes after placement)","State-based window (until shipment)","Hybrid: time-based for guaranteed cancellation, state-based for best-effort"],"recommendation":"State-based cutoff is more intuitive for customers and simpler to implement. Recommend: cancellable until shipping label is generated."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
